Tests division of `undefined` by `null` to confirm it results in `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the result of dividing `undefined` by `null`. The expression `undefined / null` evaluates to `NaN` (Not-a-Number), and the `isNaN` function confirms this by returning `true`. The `assert` function then verifies that the result of `isNaN(undefined / null)` is indeed `true`. This code provides narrow functionality, serving as a basic test to ensure that the division of `undefined` by `null` behaves as expected in JavaScript.
